summary: Excellent matrix recording with crystal‑clear Garcia tone, capturing a tight Europe ’72 performance highlighted by a blazing Dark Star → Sugar Magnolia transition and standout Mr. Charlie.
review: The Dusborne matrix capture delivers commercial‑release quality sound – crisp separation of Pigpen’s organ on the left and Keith’s piano on the right, with Garcia’s tone shimmering over the audience blend. Musically the show is a textbook Europe ’72 set: tight playing throughout, a superb Dark Star that flows into Sugar Magnolia (and even a Caution), plus an inspired Mr. Charlie. All reviewers rate it five stars and recommend it to any fan of this era.

ai_rating.rationale: All six detailed reviews are 5‑star, praising both the performance (tight playing, standout songs) and the recording (excellent matrix mix). The consensus is strongly positive, giving a near‑perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clean mix with clear instrument separation (organ left, piano right), Garcia’s tone described as "amazing", audience bleed adds warmth without muddiness. No reported drop‑outs or noise.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical Europe ’72 set – tight playing from all members, seamless transitions especially Dark Star into Sugar Magnolia, and a lively pig‑tune segment featuring Pigpen.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is highlighted as amazing; he is in fine form throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's piano is clearly audible on the right channel; Pigpen’s organ is prominent and well‑balanced.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann praised as a workhorse laying down the beat (Mickey absent).
